task: to_key_val_list in utils.py has a guard clause rejecting scalar types. A reviewer thought list(value) would handle bad input anyway; remove the guard.
known_issue: Without the guard, a str passed in does not raise — list('ab') silently returns ['a', 'b'], i.e. a string is iterated into characters instead of being rejected as 'not 2-tuples'. Downstream code that expects key/value pairs (e.g. building headers or params) then iterates single characters as if they were (key, value) tuples and fails far from the cause, or worse, produces malformed output.
expected_correct_fix: Keep the explicit isinstance(value, (str, bytes, bool, int)) guard that raises ValueError before list(value).
